gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A **content hash** that identifies a [`SelectionPolicy`] — recorded in `Meta.policy_used` so"] # [doc = " \"which policy chose this?\" is always answerable (RFC-0005 §3; ADR-003)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is a type alias for [`mycelium_core::ContentHash`] for use at selection sites."] pub type PolicyRef = ContentHash ;
gaps.reason: `type PolicyRef = ...` alias — Mycelium's `type_item` always introduces a new nominal sum type via `'=' constructor ('|' constructor)*`; a bare alias to an existing type would fabricate a sum type where none exists semantically

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Build and validate a [`SelectionPolicy`] from a name, candidates, rules, a default arm, and"] # [doc = " a cost model (C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — either the policy is accepted (total, well-formed) or it is refused"] # [doc = " with an explicit [`PolicyError`]; there is no silent completion of a non-total table."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`PolicyError::NoCandidates`] — the candidate set is empty."] # [doc = " - [`PolicyError::IndexOutOfRange`] — a rule's `Choose(i)` or the default arm points outside"] # [doc = "   the candidate list."] # [doc = " - [`PolicyError::BadCost`] — `cost.storage_weight` is non-finite or ≤ 0."] # [doc = " - [`PolicyError::BadPredicateLiteral`] — a predicate carries a non-finite `f64` literal"] # [doc = "   (would collide distinct policies under content addressing — A5-01)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Examples"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```rust"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_select::{"] # [doc = "     build, Action, Candidate, CostModel, Predicate, Rule,"] # [doc = " };"] # [doc = " use mycelium_core::{Repr, ScalarKind};"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let policy = build("] # [doc = "     \"example.v1\","] # [doc = "     vec![Candidate::Repr(Repr::Dense { dim: 128, dtype: ScalarKind::F32 })],"] # [doc = "     vec![Rule { when: Predicate::Always, action: Action::Cheapest }],"] # [doc = "     0,"] # [doc = "     CostModel { storage_weight: 1.0 },"] # [doc = " ).expect(\"well-formed policy\");"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " assert_eq!(policy.name(), \"example.v1\");"] # [doc = " ```"] pub fn build (name : impl Into < String > , candidates : Vec < Candidate > , rules : Vec < Rule > , default_choice : usize , cost : CostModel ,) -> Result < SelectionPolicy , PolicyError > { SelectionPolicy :: new (name , candidates , rules , default_choice , cost) }
gaps.reason: unsupported Rust type form `impl Into < String >`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Return the content address of a [`SelectionPolicy`] — its [`PolicyRef`] (RFC-0005 §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — total; a valid policy always has a deterministic `PolicyRef`."] # [doc = " The hash is over the policy's canonical JSON serialization (RFC-0001 §4.6; ADR-003)."] # [must_use] pub fn policy_ref (p : & SelectionPolicy) -> PolicyRef { p . policy_ref () }

Emitting bare `policy_ref(recv, …)` would fabricate an unknown prim and file-poison myc-check (G-β Rank A / G2/VR-5; residual when the method body itself gapped — e.g. M-1037 `.to_vec()` inside `read_to_end` — so the declaration was never registered)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **one selection mechanism** (RFC-0005 §4; C3): evaluate the decision table and return"] # [doc = " the chosen candidate **with its mandatory [`Explanation`]** — there is no code path that"] # [doc = " returns a choice without one."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — the policy is a total predicate over *exact* metadata (RFC-0005"] # [doc = " §2.5): same `(policy, inputs)` → same `(choice, explanation)`, deterministically."] # [doc = " The `Exact` tag covers the *selection decision*, not the accuracy of the downstream op."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`SelectError::WrongSiteKind`] — the chosen candidate does not match the expected kind at"] # [doc = "   a typed site adapter. Use [`select_swap_target`], [`select_packing`], or"] # [doc = "   [`select_decode_method`] to enforce kind at the type level."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN guarantee (C3)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The returned [`Explanation`] contains:"] # [doc = " - `inputs` — the exact [`SelectionInputs`] that were considered."] # [doc = " - `costs` — every candidate's cost in declared storage **bits** (RFC-0005 §2; M-220)."] # [doc = " - `matched_rule` — the rule index that fired, or `None` when the default arm decided."] # [doc = " - `chosen_index` / `chosen` — the selected candidate."] # [doc = " - `overridden` — `false` on this path (see [`select_with_override`])."] # [doc = " - `policy` / `policy_name` — the content address and name of the deciding policy."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Examples"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```rust"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_select::{build, select, Action, Candidate, CostModel, Predicate, Provenance, Rule};"] # [doc = " use mycelium_core::{Repr, ScalarKind, Meta};"] # [doc = " use mycelium_select::SelectionInputs;"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let policy = build("] # [doc = "     \"trivial.v1\","] # [doc = "     vec![Candidate::Repr(Repr::Dense { dim: 64, dtype: ScalarKind::F32 })],"] # [doc = "     vec![Rule { when: Predicate::Always, action: Action::Cheapest }],"] # [doc = "     0,"] # [doc = "     CostModel { storage_weight: 1.0 },"] # [doc = " ).unwrap();"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let meta = Meta::exact(Provenance::Root);"] # [doc = " let inputs = SelectionInputs::from_meta(Repr::Binary { width: 64 }, &meta);"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let (choice, explanation) = select(&policy, &inputs).unwrap();"] # [doc = " assert!(!explanation.overridden);"] # [doc = " assert_eq!(explanation.chosen, choice);"] # [doc = " ```"] pub fn select (policy : & SelectionPolicy , inputs : & SelectionInputs ,) -> Result < (Candidate , Explanation) , SelectError > { mycelium_select :: select (policy , inputs , None) }

A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **explain capability** (RFC-0005 §4): derive the mandatory [`Explanation`] for a"] # [doc = " `(policy, inputs)` pair without performing a selection — **total and deterministic** over"] # [doc = " any valid [`SelectionPolicy`] (M-221)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — total; an un-overridden selection on a validated policy cannot"] # [doc = " fail. The returned [`Explanation`] is re-derivable from `(policy, inputs)` alone — it"] # [doc = " carries the content address of the deciding policy so the record is self-describing."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN guarantee (C3)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `Explanation` *is* the artifact: it reveals which rule matched, per-candidate costs in"] # [doc = " declared bits, the chosen option, and the `PolicyRef` — answering *\"why this choice?\"* from"] # [doc = " the policy alone."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Examples"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```rust"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_select::{build, explain, policy_ref, Action, Candidate, CostModel, Predicate, Provenance, Rule};"] # [doc = " use mycelium_core::{Repr, ScalarKind, Meta};"] # [doc = " use mycelium_select::SelectionInputs;"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let policy = build("] # [doc = "     \"explain-demo.v1\","] # [doc = "     vec![Candidate::Repr(Repr::Dense { dim: 32, dtype: ScalarKind::F16 })],"] # [doc = "     vec![Rule { when: Predicate::Always, action: Action::Cheapest }],"] # [doc = "     0,"] # [doc = "     CostModel { storage_weight: 1.0 },"] # [doc = " ).unwrap();"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let meta = Meta::exact(Provenance::Root);"] # [doc = " let inputs = SelectionInputs::from_meta(Repr::Binary { width: 32 }, &meta);"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let explanation = explain(&policy, &inputs);"] # [doc = " // The explanation's policy field matches the policy's content address."] # [doc = " assert_eq!(explanation.policy, policy_ref(&policy));"] # [doc = " ```"] # [must_use] pub fn explain (policy : & SelectionPolicy , inputs : & SelectionInputs) -> Explanation { mycelium_select :: explain (policy , inputs) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A **first-class deterministic override**: force a specific candidate by index and record"] # [doc = " the override state *in* the [`Explanation`] — the overridden selection remains fully"] # [doc = " inspectable (M-221; RFC-0005 §2.4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — the forced choice is deterministic and the `Explanation` marks"] # [doc = " `overridden: true`, so no selection is ever silently coerced (C1/C3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`SelectError::OverrideOutOfRange`] — `forced_index` is outside the candidate list; this"] # [doc = "   is an explicit refusal, never a snap to the nearest legal choice (C1)."] # [doc = " - [`SelectError::WrongSiteKind`] — if the forced candidate does not fit the call site; use"] # [doc = "   the typed site adapters where kind enforcement matters."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # EXPLAIN guarantee (C3)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `explanation.overridden == true` on every successful path — the override state is recorded,"] # [doc = " never hidden."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Examples"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ```rust"] # [doc = " use mycelium_std_select::{"] # [doc = "     build, select_with_override, Action, Candidate, CostModel, Predicate, Provenance, Rule,"] # [doc = " };"] # [doc = " use mycelium_core::{Repr, ScalarKind, Meta};"] # [doc = " use mycelium_select::SelectionInputs;"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let policy = build("] # [doc = "     \"override-demo.v1\","] # [doc = "     vec!["] # [doc = "         Candidate::Repr(Repr::Binary { width: 8 }),"] # [doc = "         Candidate::Repr(Repr::Dense { dim: 8, dtype: ScalarKind::F32 }),"] # [doc = "     ],"] # [doc = "     vec![Rule { when: Predicate::Always, action: Action::Choose(0) }],"] # [doc = "     0,"] # [doc = "     CostModel { storage_weight: 1.0 },"] # [doc = " ).unwrap();"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " let meta = Meta::exact(Provenance::Root);"] # [doc = " let inputs = SelectionInputs::from_meta(Repr::Binary { width: 8 }, &meta);"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " // Force the second candidate even though the rule would choose index 0."] # [doc = " let (choice, explanation) = select_with_override(&policy, &inputs, 1).unwrap();"] # [doc = " assert!(explanation.overridden);"] # [doc = " assert_eq!(explanation.chosen_index, 1);"] # [doc = " let _ = choice;"] # [doc = " ```"] pub fn select_with_override (policy : & SelectionPolicy , inputs : & SelectionInputs , forced_index : usize ,) -> Result < (Candidate , Explanation) , SelectError > { mycelium_select :: select (policy , inputs , Some (forced_index)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One row of the guarantee matrix (RFC-0016 §4.5; spec §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The matrix is **data** — asserted in tests — not prose only. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The honest guarantee tag — C2 / VR-5."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Exact ⊐ Proven ⊐ Empirical ⊐ Declared`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All ops in `std.select` are `Exact` — the policy is a total predicate over *exact* metadata"] # [doc = " (RFC-0005 §2.5). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The loaded guarantee matrix — 5 rows, all `Exact`, EXPLAIN-able = yes for every selection op."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Asserted in unit tests; never prose only (RFC-0016 §4.5)."] pub static GUARANTEE_MATRIX : & [GuaranteeRow] = & [GuaranteeRow { op : "build" , tag : GuaranteeTag :: Exact , fallible : true , effects : "none" , explain_able : ExplainAble :: NotApplicable , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "policy_ref" , tag : GuaranteeTag :: Exact , fallible : false , effects : "none" , explain_able : ExplainAble :: NotApplicable , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "select" , tag : GuaranteeTag :: Exact , fallible : true , effects : "none" , explain_able : ExplainAble :: Yes , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "explain" , tag : GuaranteeTag :: Exact , fallible : false , effects : "none" , explain_able : ExplainAble :: Yes , } , GuaranteeRow { op : "select_with_override" , tag : GuaranteeTag :: Exact , fallible : true , effects : "none" , explain_able : ExplainAble :: Yes , } ,] ;
gaps.reason: top-level `static GUARANTEE_MATRIX` — no static item production in the grammar

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use mycelium_core :: { Meta , Provenance , Repr , ScalarKind } ; use mycelium_select :: SelectionInputs ; use super :: * ; fn exact_meta () -> Meta { Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) } fn binary_inputs (width : u32) -> SelectionInputs { SelectionInputs :: from_meta (Repr :: Binary { width } , & exact_meta ()) } fn dense_inputs (dim : u32) -> SelectionInputs { SelectionInputs :: from_meta (Repr :: Dense { dim , dtype : ScalarKind :: F32 , } , & exact_meta () ,) } fn one_repr_policy (name : & str , repr : Repr) -> SelectionPolicy { build (name , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (repr)] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: Always , action : Action :: Cheapest , }] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . expect ("well-formed policy") } fn two_repr_policy () -> SelectionPolicy { build ("two-repr.v1" , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 }) , Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Dense { dim : 8 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F32 , }) ,] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: Always , action : Action :: Cheapest , }] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . expect ("well-formed two-repr policy") } # [doc = " GUARANTEE_MATRIX has exactly 5 rows, covering every exported op."] # [test] fn matrix_has_five_rows () { assert_eq ! (GUARANTEE_MATRIX . len () , 5 , "matrix must have exactly 5 rows (one per exported op)") ; } # [doc = " Every matrix row carries the `Exact` tag — the honest tag for a total predicate over"] # [doc = " exact metadata (spec §4 \"Tag justification\"; VR-5)."] # [test] fn matrix_all_rows_exact () { for row in GUARANTEE_MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . tag , GuaranteeTag :: Exact , "op `{}` must be tagged Exact (VR-5 — total predicate over exact metadata)" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " Every selection op in the matrix is EXPLAIN-able = yes (C3 crux)."] # [test] fn matrix_selection_ops_explain_able () { let selection_ops = ["select" , "explain" , "select_with_override"] ; for op_name in selection_ops { let row = GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == op_name) . unwrap_or_else (| | panic ! ("matrix missing row for op `{op_name}`")) ; assert_eq ! (row . explain_able , ExplainAble :: Yes , "op `{op_name}` must be EXPLAIN-able = yes (C3 crux)") ; } } # [doc = " Non-selection ops (`build`, `policy_ref`) are not themselves selections."] # [test] fn matrix_non_selection_ops_not_applicable () { let non_selection_ops = ["build" , "policy_ref"] ; for op_name in non_selection_ops { let row = GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == op_name) . unwrap_or_else (| | panic ! ("matrix missing row for op `{op_name}`")) ; assert_eq ! (row . explain_able , ExplainAble :: NotApplicable , "op `{op_name}` is not a selection; explain_able must be NotApplicable") ; } } # [doc = " Every op in the matrix declares no effects (C6 — pure functions)."] # [test] fn matrix_all_ops_no_effects () { for row in GUARANTEE_MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . effects , "none" , "op `{}` must declare no effects (C6 — pure)" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " Fallibility matches spec §4: `build`, `select`, `select_with_override` are fallible;"] # [doc = " `policy_ref` and `explain` are total."] # [test] fn matrix_fallibility_matches_spec () { let fallible_ops = ["build" , "select" , "select_with_override"] ; let total_ops = ["policy_ref" , "explain"] ; for op in fallible_ops { let row = GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == op) . unwrap () ; assert ! (row . fallible , "op `{op}` should be fallible per spec §4") ; } for op in total_ops { let row = GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . find (| r | r . op == op) . unwrap () ; assert ! (! row . fallible , "op `{op}` should be total (non-fallible) per spec §4") ; } } # [doc = " `build` refuses an empty candidate set with the exact `NoCandidates` variant (C1)."] # [test] fn build_refuses_empty_candidates () { let err = build ("empty" , vec ! [] , vec ! [] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . expect_err ("empty candidate set must be refused") ; assert_eq ! (err , PolicyError :: NoCandidates , "must be NoCandidates, not a silent default") ; } # [doc = " `build` refuses a rule whose `Choose(i)` is out of range (C1 — never a silent clamp)."] # [test] fn build_refuses_out_of_range_index () { let err = build ("bad-index" , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 1 })] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: Always , action : Action :: Choose (99) , }] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . expect_err ("out-of-range Choose index must be refused") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , PolicyError :: IndexOutOfRange { index : 99 }) , "must be IndexOutOfRange {{index: 99}}, got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " `build` refuses a non-positive cost weight (C1 — never a silent clamp)."] # [test] fn build_refuses_bad_cost () { let err = build ("bad-cost" , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 1 })] , vec ! [] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : - 1.0 , } ,) . expect_err ("non-positive cost weight must be refused") ; assert_eq ! (err , PolicyError :: BadCost , "must be BadCost, not silent") ; } # [doc = " `build` refuses a non-finite float literal in a predicate (A5-01 content-addressing"] # [doc = " safety — two policies with NaN/∞ literals would collide on the same PolicyRef)."] # [test] fn build_refuses_non_finite_predicate_literal () { let err = build ("nan-pred" , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 1 })] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: ErrorEpsAtMost (f64 :: NAN) , action : Action :: Choose (0) , }] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . expect_err ("non-finite predicate literal must be refused") ; assert_eq ! (err , PolicyError :: BadPredicateLiteral , "must be BadPredicateLiteral (A5-01 — NaN collapses content identity)") ; } # [doc = " `select` always returns an `Explanation`; its `policy` field matches the policy's"] # [doc = " `policy_ref` (C3 + C4 — content-addressed, self-describing)."] # [test] fn select_emits_explanation_with_correct_policy_ref () { let policy = one_repr_policy ("explain-check.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 32 }) ; let inputs = binary_inputs (32) ; let (_ , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . expect ("select must succeed") ; assert_eq ! (explanation . policy , policy_ref (& policy) , "explanation.policy must match policy_ref (C4 — content-addressed)") ; } # [doc = " `select` emits `overridden: false` on a normal (non-overridden) selection."] # [test] fn select_emits_not_overridden () { let policy = one_repr_policy ("no-override.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 16 }) ; let inputs = binary_inputs (16) ; let (_ , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert ! (! explanation . overridden , "normal select must not set overridden=true (C3 — override state recorded faithfully)") ; } # [doc = " `explain` always returns a valid Explanation; the policy field matches `policy_ref` (C3)."] # [test] fn explain_emits_explanation_with_correct_policy_ref () { let policy = one_repr_policy ("explain-op.v1" , Repr :: Dense { dim : 64 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F16 , } ,) ; let inputs = dense_inputs (64) ; let explanation = explain (& policy , & inputs) ; assert_eq ! (explanation . policy , policy_ref (& policy) , "explain.policy must match policy_ref (C3 + C4)") ; } # [doc = " `select_with_override` records `overridden: true` in the Explanation — the override"] # [doc = " state is never hidden (C3; RFC-0005 §2.4)."] # [test] fn select_with_override_records_override_in_explanation () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let (_ , explanation) = select_with_override (& policy , & inputs , 1) . unwrap () ; assert ! (explanation . overridden , "select_with_override must set overridden=true (C3 — override state recorded)") ; assert_eq ! (explanation . chosen_index , 1 , "forced index must be the chosen index") ; } # [doc = " `select_with_override` refuses an out-of-range forced index with `OverrideOutOfRange`"] # [doc = " — never snaps to the nearest legal choice (C1)."] # [test] fn select_with_override_refuses_out_of_range () { let policy = one_repr_policy ("override-range.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 4 }) ; let inputs = binary_inputs (4) ; let err = select_with_override (& policy , & inputs , 99) . expect_err ("out-of-range override must be refused") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , SelectError :: OverrideOutOfRange { index : 99 , candidates : 1 }) , "must be OverrideOutOfRange {{index: 99, candidates: 1}}, got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " `select` is deterministic: same `(policy, inputs)` → same `(choice, explanation)`."] # [doc = " Exhaustive over a small candidate set."] # [test] fn select_is_deterministic () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let (choice_a , expl_a) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; let (choice_b , expl_b) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (choice_a , choice_b , "select must be deterministic (C2/C4)") ; assert_eq ! (expl_a , expl_b , "explanation must be deterministic (same inputs → same record)") ; } # [doc = " `explain` is deterministic: same `(policy, inputs)` → same `Explanation`."] # [test] fn explain_is_deterministic () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = dense_inputs (8) ; let expl_a = explain (& policy , & inputs) ; let expl_b = explain (& policy , & inputs) ; assert_eq ! (expl_a , expl_b , "explain must be deterministic (C4)") ; } # [doc = " `select_with_override` is deterministic for the same forced index."] # [test] fn select_with_override_is_deterministic () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let (choice_a , expl_a) = select_with_override (& policy , & inputs , 1) . unwrap () ; let (choice_b , expl_b) = select_with_override (& policy , & inputs , 1) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (choice_a , choice_b , "select_with_override must be deterministic (C2/C4)") ; assert_eq ! (expl_a , expl_b , "explanation must be deterministic") ; } # [doc = " Two policies built from identical data have the same `PolicyRef` (ADR-003 / C4)."] # [test] fn identical_policies_have_same_policy_ref () { let p1 = one_repr_policy ("content-id.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 8 }) ; let p2 = one_repr_policy ("content-id.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 8 }) ; assert_eq ! (policy_ref (& p1) , policy_ref (& p2) , "identical policies must have the same PolicyRef (ADR-003)") ; } # [doc = " Two policies with different names have different `PolicyRef`s."] # [test] fn different_policies_have_different_policy_ref () { let p1 = one_repr_policy ("name-a.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 8 }) ; let p2 = one_repr_policy ("name-b.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 8 }) ; assert_ne ! (policy_ref (& p1) , policy_ref (& p2) , "policies with different names must have different PolicyRefs") ; } # [doc = " The `PolicyRef` in an Explanation matches `policy_ref(&policy)` — the policy is always"] # [doc = " recoverable from the record alone (RFC-0005 §3; C4)."] # [test] fn explanation_policy_ref_matches_policy () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let explanation = explain (& policy , & inputs) ; assert_eq ! (explanation . policy , policy_ref (& policy) , "Explanation.policy must equal policy_ref (RFC-0005 §3 — self-describing record)") ; } # [doc = " Every candidate appears in `explanation.costs` — the EXPLAIN record is complete, not"] # [doc = " just the winner (RFC-0005 §2.2 — full ranking, not just the chosen cost)."] # [test] fn explanation_costs_covers_all_candidates () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let (_ , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (explanation . costs . len () , policy . candidates () . len () , "costs must have one entry per candidate (C3 — complete ranking)") ; } # [doc = " The `explanation.chosen` matches `explanation.costs[chosen_index]` — self-consistent."] # [test] fn explanation_chosen_consistent_with_costs () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let (_ , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (explanation . chosen , explanation . costs [explanation . chosen_index] . candidate , "chosen candidate must be consistent with chosen_index in costs") ; } # [doc = " `explain` and `select` produce consistent explanations for the same `(policy, inputs)`."] # [test] fn explain_and_select_consistent () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = dense_inputs (8) ; let (_ , select_expl) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; let explain_expl = explain (& policy , & inputs) ; assert_eq ! (select_expl , explain_expl , "explain and select must produce identical Explanation for the same inputs") ; } # [doc = " `Cheapest` selects the lower-cost candidate; costs are in declared storage bits."] # [doc = " Binary{width=8} costs 8 bits; Dense{dim=8, F32} costs 256 bits → Binary wins."] # [test] fn cheapest_selects_minimum_cost_candidate () { let policy = two_repr_policy () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (8) ; let (choice , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (choice , Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 }) , "Cheapest must select Binary (8 bits) over Dense F32 (256 bits)") ; let binary_cost = explanation . costs . iter () . find (| c | c . candidate == Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 })) . expect ("Binary must appear in costs") ; let dense_cost = explanation . costs . iter () . find (| c | { c . candidate == Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Dense { dim : 8 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F32 , }) }) . expect ("Dense must appear in costs") ; assert ! (binary_cost . cost < dense_cost . cost , "Binary ({} bits) must cost less than Dense F32 ({} bits)" , binary_cost . cost , dense_cost . cost) ; } # [doc = " Rule predicates fire in table order (first match wins — RFC-0005 §2.3 fixed precedence)."] # [test] fn predicate_rule_first_match_wins () { let policy = build ("first-match.v1" , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 1 }) , Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Dense { dim : 1 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F32 , }) ,] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: SrcKindIs (ParadigmKind :: Binary) , action : Action :: Choose (0) , } , Rule { when : Predicate :: Always , action : Action :: Choose (1) , } ,] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . unwrap () ; let inputs = binary_inputs (1) ; let (_ , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (explanation . matched_rule , Some (0) , "first matching rule must win (fixed declared precedence)") ; assert_eq ! (explanation . chosen_index , 0 , "rule 0 chooses index 0") ; } # [doc = " Default arm fires when no rule matches."] # [test] fn default_arm_fires_when_no_rule_matches () { let policy = build ("default-arm.v1" , vec ! [Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Binary { width : 1 }) , Candidate :: Repr (Repr :: Dense { dim : 1 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F32 , }) ,] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: SrcKindIs (ParadigmKind :: Binary) , action : Action :: Choose (0) , } ,] , 1 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . unwrap () ; let inputs = dense_inputs (1) ; let (_ , explanation) = select (& policy , & inputs) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (explanation . matched_rule , None , "no rule matched — default arm should fire (matched_rule = None)") ; assert_eq ! (explanation . chosen_index , 1 , "default arm chose index 1 (Dense)") ; } # [doc = " A registered policy is retrievable by its `PolicyRef` — the operational form of RFC-0005"] # [doc = " §3 (\"which policy chose this?\")."] # [test] fn policy_registry_roundtrip () { let mut registry = PolicyRegistry :: new () ; let policy = one_repr_policy ("registry-test.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 32 }) ; let pref = registry . register (policy . clone ()) ; let retrieved = registry . get (& pref) . expect ("registered policy must be retrievable") ; assert_eq ! (policy_ref (retrieved) , pref , "retrieved policy's content hash must match the registered PolicyRef") ; } # [doc = " An unregistered `PolicyRef` returns `None` — the registry never fabricates a policy."] # [test] fn policy_registry_missing_returns_none () { let registry = PolicyRegistry :: new () ; let fake_ref = policy_ref (& one_repr_policy ("ghost.v1" , Repr :: Binary { width : 1 })) ; assert ! (registry . get (& fake_ref) . is_none () , "unregistered PolicyRef must return None (C1 — never a silent fabrication)") ; } # [doc = " `select_swap_target` refuses a packing candidate at the repr site (`WrongSiteKind` — C1)."] # [test] fn swap_target_adapter_refuses_packing_candidate () { use mycelium_core :: PackScheme ; let policy = build ("mixed-kinds.v1" , vec ! [Candidate :: Packing (PackScheme :: I2S)] , vec ! [Rule { when : Predicate :: Always , action : Action :: Choose (0) , }] , 0 , CostModel { storage_weight : 1.0 , } ,) . unwrap () ; let meta = exact_meta () ; let inputs = SelectionInputs :: from_meta (Repr :: Ternary { trits : 4 } , & meta) ; let err = select_swap_target (& policy , & inputs , None) . expect_err ("Packing candidate must be refused at the swap-target site") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , SelectError :: WrongSiteKind { site : "swap-target" , .. }) , "must be WrongSiteKind {{site: swap-target}}, got {err:?}") ; } # [doc = " The built-in `bitnet_packing_policy` is well-formed and selects a packing candidate."] # [test] fn bitnet_packing_policy_selects_packing () { let policy = bitnet_packing_policy () ; let meta = exact_meta () ; let inputs = SelectionInputs :: from_meta (Repr :: Ternary { trits : 64 } , & meta) ; let (scheme , explanation) = select_packing (& policy , & inputs , None) . expect ("bitnet_packing_policy must select a valid packing") ; assert ! (BITNET_PACKINGS . contains (& scheme) , "selected packing must be in BITNET_PACKINGS") ; assert_eq ! (explanation . policy , policy_ref (& policy) , "packing explanation policy must match the policy ref (C4)") ; } # [doc = " `select_layout` refuses a non-ternary source (A5-02 well-formedness; C1 — never a"] # [doc = " silent mis-tag)."] # [test] fn select_layout_refuses_non_ternary_source () { let policy = bitnet_packing_policy () ; let meta = exact_meta () ; let inputs = SelectionInputs :: from_meta (Repr :: Binary { width : 8 } , & meta) ; let err = select_layout (& policy , & inputs , None) . expect_err ("non-ternary source must be refused at the layout site") ; assert ! (matches ! (err , SelectError :: NonTernarySource { .. }) , "must be NonTernarySource, got {err:?}") ; } }
